title: Return :snooze instead of raising on rate-limit
prompt: The worker in `test_fixtures/rate_limited_worker.ex` raises a string exception when Stripe responds with a 429. Raising wastes an attempt and pollutes the error tracker. Rewrite the 429 clause to return `{:snooze, retry_after}` using the retry_after value from the error struct. Keep the happy path and other error branches as-is. Write the result to `lib/my_app/workers/stripe_sync_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Single clause rewrite once `{:snooze, seconds}` is known; the idiom is documented in Oban.Worker's recipe section.
research_citation: Oban.Worker HexDocs — 'When a worker receives a 429 (rate limited) status code, it should return {:snooze, calculate_backoff(attempt)}.'
